page: Amazon Web Services inf2.xlarge is a Nitro-based virtual machine utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It is powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at a base frequency of 3.6 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 4 vCPUs, which map to 2 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. System memory consists of 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage and relies on network-attached storage, while baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core benchmarks placing it in the top 25% of tested servers. However, multi-core scaling and memory subsystem performance are weaker. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached reads and writes fall into the bottom 25%, which limits throughput for memory-intensive operations. Similarly, multi-threaded decompression and database operations show below-average performance.

For specialized workloads, the server handles small-scale language model inference reasonably well, achieving average tier speeds of 382.54 tokens per second for prompt processing on a 135M parameter model. Performance degrades significantly on larger models, with prompt processing for a 7B model falling into the bottom 10%. Consequently, this instance is best suited for general-purpose workloads, single-threaded applications, and lightweight inference tasks rather than memory-bound databases or large-scale machine learning workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services inf2.xlarge is a Nitro-based instance featuring a dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 4 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 3.6 GHz, it delivers strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% on stress-ng benchmarks. However, it exhibits weak memory bandwidth and multi-core scaling, with Geekbench multi-core and large-block memory read/write speeds falling into the bottom 25%. The instance lacks local storage and provides a baseline network speed of 2 Gbps. It is qualitatively cost-effective for workloads requiring dedicated single-core compute and moderate memory capacity, such as lightweight single-threaded applications, basic text processing, and small-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services inf2.xlarge is a Nitro-based instance with a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC CPU, 4 vCPUs, and 16 GB RAM, offering strong single-core performance but limited memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services inf2.xlarge features 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC CPU, delivering strong single-core performance.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC instance offering strong single-core compute and balanced memory for lightweight inference and general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz
16 GB of DDR4 memory with a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Strong single-core CPU performance in the top 25% of benchmarks
Average performance for small-scale 135M parameter LLM inference
Constrained by weak memory bandwidth and lack of local storage
Best suited for single-threaded applications and lightweight workloads
